metadata.description: Form 1065 (U.S. Return of Partnership Income) intake questions. Covers entity info, partners, guaranteed payments, income, deductions, balance sheet, capital accounts, reconciliation, K-1 allocations, and at-risk/passive activity rules.

sections.title: Partnership Information
sections.description: Basic partnership identification and formation details

questions.text: Legal name of the partnership
Employer Identification Number (EIN)
Date the partnership was formed
State of formation
Principal business address
Principal business activity description
questions.helpText: Describe the primary business activity (e.g., 'Real Estate Investment', 'Medical Practice', 'Consulting')

questions.text: Business activity code (NAICS)
questions.helpText: 6-digit code from the Principal Business Activity Codes list

questions.text: Type of partnership
Does the partnership use a fiscal year end other than December 31?
Fiscal year end date
Business phone number
Total number of partners at end of tax year
Is this partnership a partner in another partnership?

sections.title: Partner Information
sections.description: Details for each partner. Complete for all partners who held an interest at any time during the tax year.

questions.text: Partner's full name (or entity name)
Is this partner an individual or an entity?
Partner's Social Security Number
Partner's EIN
Partner's address
Partner type
Ownership percentage
Profit sharing percentage
Loss sharing percentage
Capital sharing percentage
Beginning capital account balance
Ending capital account balance
Is this partner a foreign partner?
Did this partner's ownership interest change during the year?
Does this partner have any loans to or from the partnership?

sections.title: Accounting Method & Tax Year
sections.description: Accounting method and tax year information for the partnership

questions.text: What accounting method does the partnership use?
Describe the accounting method
Does the partnership use a calendar year (January 1 - December 31)?
Fiscal year beginning date
Fiscal year ending date
Did the partnership change its accounting method during the tax year?
Was Form 3115 (Application for Change in Accounting Method) filed?
Section 481(a) adjustment amount, if any
questions.helpText: The adjustment required when changing accounting methods

sections.title: Gross Receipts / Income
sections.description: Total income items reported on Form 1065 page 1

questions.text: Gross receipts or sales
questions.helpText: Total revenue from the sale of goods or services before any deductions

questions.text: Did the partnership have any returns and allowances?
Total returns and allowances
Does the partnership sell products or have inventory?
questions.helpText: If yes, Cost of Goods Sold section will need to be completed

questions.text: Ordinary income (loss) from other partnerships, estates, and trusts
Net farm profit (loss) — Form 4835 or Schedule F
Net gain (loss) from Form 4797 (sale of business property)
Did the partnership have any other income (interest, rents, royalties, etc.)?
Total other income
Description of other income
Did the partnership receive any 1099 forms?
Please upload all 1099 forms received

sections.title: Cost of Goods Sold
sections.description: For partnerships that sell products or maintain inventory (Schedule A of Form 1065)

questions.text: What inventory valuation method does the partnership use?
Describe the inventory valuation method
Was there any change in determining quantities, costs, or valuations between opening and closing inventory?
Inventory at beginning of year
Purchases less cost of items withdrawn for personal use
Cost of labor
Additional Section 263A costs
questions.helpText: Uniform capitalization (UNICAP) costs required to be included in inventory under Section 263A

questions.text: Other costs
Description of other costs
Inventory at end of year
Does the partnership use the LIFO inventory method?

sections.title: Deductions
sections.description: Deductible business expenses reported on Form 1065 page 1 (Lines 9-21)

questions.text: Salaries and wages (excluding guaranteed payments to partners)
questions.helpText: W-2 wages paid to non-partner employees only

questions.text: Guaranteed payments to partners
questions.helpText: Total guaranteed payments for services and use of capital (detail entered in Guaranteed Payments section)

questions.text: Repairs and maintenance
Bad debts
questions.helpText: Debts owed to the partnership that became worthless during the year

questions.text: Rents paid
Taxes and licenses
questions.helpText: State and local taxes, payroll taxes, business licenses, and permits (not federal income tax)

questions.text: Interest expense
Depreciation (from Form 4562, if required)
Depletion (other than oil and gas)
Retirement plans and employee benefit programs
Employee benefit programs (health insurance, life insurance, dependent care, etc.)
Did the partnership have any other deductions not listed above?
Description of other deduction
Amount of other deduction

sections.title: Depreciation & Asset Dispositions
sections.description: Form 4562 depreciation and amortization, plus asset sales (Form 4797)

questions.text: Did the partnership purchase any new business assets during the tax year?
Description of new asset
Date new asset was placed in service
Cost or other basis of new asset
Business use percentage of new asset
Does the partnership wish to elect Section 179 expensing for any assets?
questions.helpText: Section 179 allows immediate expensing of qualifying property up to the annual limit. The deduction passes through to partners.

questions.text: Total Section 179 election amount
Does the partnership have existing assets from prior years still being depreciated?
Please upload the depreciation schedule from the prior year return
Did the partnership dispose of (sell, trade, abandon, or involuntarily convert) any business assets during the year?
Description of disposed asset
Date the asset was disposed of
Sales price or amount realized on disposition

sections.title: Guaranteed Payments to Partners
sections.description: Payments to partners for services rendered or use of capital, determined without regard to partnership income. Repeatable per partner.

questions.text: Partner name receiving guaranteed payments
Guaranteed payments for services
questions.helpText: Payments to the partner for services rendered to the partnership, regardless of partnership income

questions.text: Guaranteed payments for use of capital
questions.helpText: Payments to the partner for the use of capital contributed to the partnership

questions.text: Are the guaranteed payments for services subject to self-employment tax?
questions.helpText: Generally, guaranteed payments to a general partner or LLC member-manager for services are subject to SE tax

questions.text: Were health insurance premiums paid on behalf of this partner included in guaranteed payments?
Amount of health insurance premiums included
Were retirement plan contributions made on behalf of this partner?
Amount of retirement plan contributions

sections.title: Balance Sheet (Schedule L)
sections.description: Beginning and end of year balance sheet. Required if total receipts and total assets are both $250,000 or more.

questions.text: Cash and cash equivalents — beginning of year
Cash and cash equivalents — end of year
Accounts receivable (net) — beginning of year
Accounts receivable (net) — end of year
Inventories — beginning of year
Inventories — end of year
Other current assets — beginning of year
Other current assets — end of year
Loans to partners — beginning of year
Loans to partners — end of year
Other assets (net of depreciation) — beginning of year
questions.helpText: Includes fixed assets, intangibles, and other long-term assets net of accumulated depreciation

questions.text: Other assets (net of depreciation) — end of year
Accounts payable — beginning of year
Accounts payable — end of year
Other current liabilities — beginning of year
Other current liabilities — end of year
Loans from partners — beginning of year
Loans from partners — end of year
Other liabilities — beginning of year
Other liabilities — end of year
Partners' capital accounts — beginning of year
Partners' capital accounts — end of year
Please upload the year-end balance sheet or trial balance from your accounting system

sections.title: Book-to-Tax Reconciliation (Schedule M-1 / M-2)
sections.description: Reconciliation of income per books with income per return, and analysis of partners' capital accounts. Required when total assets are $250,000 or more.

questions.text: Net income (loss) per books
Income included on Schedule K not recorded on books this year
questions.helpText: e.g., tax-exempt interest recorded on the return but not in book income

questions.text: Guaranteed payments to partners (per books)
Expenses recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
questions.helpText: e.g., book depreciation exceeding tax depreciation, non-deductible expenses

questions.text: Description of book expenses not deducted on return
Income recorded on books this year not included on Schedule K
questions.helpText: e.g., tax-exempt interest, life insurance proceeds

questions.text: Deductions included on Schedule K not charged against books this year
questions.helpText: e.g., tax depreciation exceeding book depreciation

questions.text: Schedule M-2: Balance at beginning of year (all partners' capital accounts)
Schedule M-2: Capital contributed during the year
Schedule M-2: Net income (loss) per return
Schedule M-2: Other increases (describe)
Schedule M-2: Other increases amount
Schedule M-2: Distributions to partners
Schedule M-2: Other decreases (describe)
Schedule M-2: Other decreases amount

sections.title: Partner Capital Accounts
sections.description: Individual partner capital account analysis. Repeatable per partner. Reported on Schedule K-1 Item L.

questions.text: Partner name
Beginning capital account balance
Capital contributed during the year
Current year increase (share of income)
Current year decrease (share of loss)
Withdrawals and distributions
Ending capital account balance
What method is used to report the capital account?
Describe the capital account reporting method
Does this partner's share of liabilities include any recourse debt?
Partner's share of recourse liabilities
Partner's share of nonrecourse liabilities
Partner's share of qualified nonrecourse financing
questions.helpText: Qualified nonrecourse financing from real property activities increases the partner's at-risk amount

sections.title: Schedule K-1 Allocation
sections.description: How income, loss, deductions, and credits are allocated among partners, including Section 199A QBI and self-employment earnings

questions.text: How are income, loss, deductions, and credits allocated among partners?

options.label: Special allocations per the partnership agreement

questions.text: Please upload the partnership agreement or relevant allocation provisions
Do the special allocations have substantial economic effect under Section 704(b)?
questions.helpText: Allocations that lack substantial economic effect will be reallocated according to partners' interests in the partnership

questions.text: Does the partnership have qualified business income (QBI) under Section 199A?
questions.helpText: QBI is generally net income from a qualified trade or business. Certain service businesses (SSTBs) may be limited.

questions.text: Is this business a specified service trade or business (SSTB)?
questions.helpText: SSTBs include health, law, accounting, actuarial, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage, and businesses where the principal asset is the reputation or skill of employees

questions.text: Total W-2 wages paid by the qualified business
questions.helpText: Used for the W-2 wage limitation on the Section 199A deduction

questions.text: Unadjusted basis immediately after acquisition (UBIA) of qualified property
questions.helpText: The original cost basis of tangible depreciable property used in the business, still within the applicable recovery period

questions.text: Does the partnership have net earnings from self-employment to report to partners?
questions.helpText: General partners and LLC member-managers typically have SE income; limited partners generally do not (except for guaranteed payments for services)

questions.text: Does the partnership have any separately stated items that must be reported on K-1?
questions.helpText: e.g., charitable contributions, Section 1231 gains, foreign transactions, rental income, portfolio income

questions.text: Total charitable contributions made by the partnership
Did the partnership have any tax-exempt income?
Total tax-exempt income
Non-deductible expenses (other than distributions)
questions.helpText: e.g., 50% of meals, penalties, fines, political contributions

sections.title: At-Risk & Passive Activity Rules
sections.description: At-risk limitations under Section 465, passive activity rules under Section 469, and material participation tests for partners

questions.text: Is the partnership engaged in any activity subject to at-risk limitations under Section 465?
questions.helpText: Most business activities are subject to at-risk rules. A partner's loss is limited to the amount they have at risk in the activity.

questions.text: Does the partnership have any nonrecourse debt that is not qualified nonrecourse financing?
questions.helpText: Nonrecourse debt (other than qualified nonrecourse financing for real property) reduces a partner's at-risk amount

questions.text: Is the partnership's activity classified as a passive activity for any partners?
questions.helpText: A passive activity is a trade or business in which the taxpayer does not materially participate, or any rental activity

questions.text: Is any of the partnership's income from a rental activity?
questions.helpText: Rental activities are generally treated as passive regardless of material participation

questions.text: If rental activity: does any partner qualify as a real estate professional?
questions.helpText: A real estate professional may treat rental real estate activities as non-passive if they materially participate

questions.text: Which material participation test do the general partners/managing members meet?
questions.helpText: There are seven tests for material participation. Select the most applicable.

options.label: Participated more than 500 hours during the year
Participation was substantially all of the participation in the activity
Participated more than 100 hours and not less than any other individual
Significant participation activities totaling more than 500 hours
Materially participated in 5 of the prior 10 years
Personal service activity — participated in any 3 prior years
Regular, continuous, and substantial participation based on facts and circumstances

questions.text: Does the partnership have any limited partners who are not material participants?
questions.helpText: Limited partners generally cannot materially participate except under the 500-hour, 5-of-10-year, or personal service tests

questions.text: Does the partnership have any suspended passive activity losses from prior years?
Were any partnership interests disposed of during the year (which would release suspended passive losses)?
Does the partnership group multiple activities together under the activity grouping rules?
questions.helpText: Partnerships may elect to group activities as a single activity for purposes of the passive activity rules

questions.text: Please describe the activity groupings

sections.title: State Filing Requirements
sections.description: State income tax and filing obligations for the partnership

questions.text: Include shared state-nexus questions
Does the partnership file composite returns on behalf of non-resident partners in any state?
questions.helpText: Some states require or allow partnerships to file a composite return and pay tax on behalf of non-resident partners

questions.text: Select states where composite returns are filed
Is the partnership subject to any state-level entity tax or franchise tax?
questions.helpText: Some states impose an entity-level tax on partnerships (e.g., California annual tax, New York filing fee)

questions.text: Select states where entity-level or franchise tax applies
Did the partnership elect to pay a pass-through entity tax (PTET) in any state?
questions.helpText: Many states now allow partnerships to elect to pay an entity-level tax that provides a SALT cap workaround for partners

questions.text: Select states where PTET election was made
Total PTET payments made during the tax year
Were state estimated tax payments made during the year?
Please upload documentation of state estimated tax payments
Does the partnership have any state withholding obligations for non-resident partners?
